papers.title: Efficacy and safety of once-daily oral orforglipron compared with oral semaglutide in adults with type 2 diabetes (ACHIEVE-3): a multinational, multicentre, non-inferiority, open-label, randomised, phase 3 trial
papers.authors: Julio Rosenstock, Daisuke Yabe, David Cox, et al.
papers.abstract_en:
Background Orforglipron is a novel non-peptide (GLP-1) receptor agonist designed for daily oral administration without food or water restrictions. This study aimed to compare the efficacy and safety of orforglipron with oral semaglutide in individuals with type 2 diabetes inadequately controlled with metformin.
Methods In this 52-week, randomised, open-label, active-controlled, multicentre, multinational, phase 3 study, we enrolled adults (≥18 years) with type 2 diabetes inadequately controlled with metformin (≥1500 mg per day), glycated haemoglobin (HbA1c) between 7.0% and 10.5% (53–91 mmol/mol), and BMI at least 25 kg/m² from 131 medical research centres and hospitals in Argentina, China, Japan, Mexico, and the USA. Participants were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) to orforglipron (12 mg or 36 mg) or semaglutide (7 mg or 14 mg); all groups had an up to 4-week lead-in period and 52-week treatment period, with the drugs administered orally once per day. The primary objective of the study was to assess non-inferiority of orforglipron 36 mg versus semaglutide 14 mg and orforglipron 12 mg versus semaglutide 7 mg for mean change at week 52 from baseline in HbA1c (with a non-inferiority margin of 0.3%) in the intention-to-treat population. Hierarchical analysis for superiority was prespecified after attainment of non-inferiority. The treatment regimen estimand, based on data from all randomly assigned participants regardless of intercurrent events, was the primary estimand; the efficacy estimand was considered supportive. The safety endpoints used data from all participants who received at least one dose of the study drug. This trial was registered on ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT06045221) and is completed.
Findings From Sept 22, 2023, to Aug 22, 2025, 1698 participants were recruited and randomly assigned to orforglipron (n=424 on 12 mg and n=423 on 36 mg) or semaglutide (n=426 on 7 mg and n=425 on 14 mg). For the treatment regimen estimand, mean changes at week 52 from a baseline HbA1c of 8.3% were –1.71% (SE 0.07) with orforglipron 12 mg, –1.91% (0.08) with orforglipron 36 mg, –1.23% (0.05) with semaglutide 7 mg, and –1.47% (0.06) with semaglutide 14 mg. Estimated treatment differences were –0.48% (95% CI –0.65 to –0.31; p<0.0001) for orforglipron 12 mg versus semaglutide 7 mg; –0.44% (–0.62 to –0·26; p<0.0001) for orforglipron 36 mg versus semaglutide 14 mg; –0.24% (95% CI –0.41 to –0.072; p=0.0050) for orforglipron 12 mg versus semaglutide 14 mg; and –0.68% (–0.85 to –0.50; p<0.0001) for orforglipron 36 mg versus semaglutide 7 mg. The primary objective of non-inferiority was met and both orforglipron doses showed superiority to both semaglutide doses, including orforglipron 12 mg versus semaglutide 14 mg. The most frequent adverse events were gastrointestinal events (orforglipron: 249 [59%] of 424 on 12 mg and 245 [58%] of 423 on 36 mg; semaglutide: 157 [37%] of 426 on 7 mg and 193 [45%] of 425 on 14 mg), most of which were mild to moderate in severity. More participants in the orforglipron groups (37 [9%] on 12 mg and 41 [10%] on 36 mg) discontinued study treatment due to adverse events than in the semaglutide groups (19 (4%) on 7 mg and 21 (5%) on 14 mg), and mean increase in pulse rate was greater in the orforglipron groups (12 mg 3.7 bpm; 36 mg 4.7 bpm) than in the semaglutide groups (7 mg 1.0 bpm; 14 mg 1.5 bpm). Four deaths occurred during the study: one in the orforglipron 12 mg group, one in the orforglipron 36 mg group, and two in the semaglutide 7 mg group.
Interpretation In individuals with type 2 diabetes inadequately controlled with metformin, orforglipron 12 mg and 36 mg was non-inferior and superior to semaglutide 7 mg and 14 mg with respect to the mean change in HbA1c from baseline to 52 weeks. Although the safety profiles of both orforglipron and semaglutide were generally consistent with the GLP-1 receptor agonist class, the incidence of gastrointestinal events, discontinuations due to adverse events, and mean increase in pulse rate were higher with orforglipron than oral semaglutide.

abstract_sections.label: 背景
abstract_sections.content: オルフログリプロンは、食事や水の制限なしに毎日経口投与できるように設計された新規の非ペプチド（GLP-1）受容体作動薬である。本研究は、メトホルミンで不十分にコントロールされている2型糖尿病患者において、オルフログリプロンの有効性と安全性と経口セマグルチドを比較することを目的とした。
abstract_sections.content_en: Orforglipron is a novel non-peptide (GLP-1) receptor agonist designed for daily oral administration without food or water restrictions. This study aimed to compare the efficacy and safety of orforglipron with oral semaglutide in individuals with type 2 diabetes inadequately controlled with metformin.

abstract_sections.label: 方法
abstract_sections.content: この52週間のランダム化非盲検実薬対照多施設多国籍第3相試験では、メトホルミン（1日1500mg以上）で不十分にコントロールされた2型糖尿病患者（HbA1c 7.0%～10.5% [53～91 mmol/mol]、BMI 25 kg/m²以上）をアルゼンチン、中国、日本、メキシコ、米国の131の医療研究施設および病院から登録した。参加者はオルフログリプロン（12 mgまたは36 mg）またはセマグルチド（7 mgまたは14 mg）にランダムに割り付けられ（1:1:1:1）、すべての群で最大4週間の導入期間と52週間の治療期間が設けられ、薬剤は1日1回経口投与された。本研究の主要目的は、HbA1cのベースラインからの52週時点での平均変化に関して、オルフログリプロン36 mg対セマグルチド14 mgおよびオルフログリプロン12 mg対セマグルチド7 mgの非劣性（非劣性マージン0.3%）をintention-to-treat集団で評価することであった。優越性の階層的解析は非劣性達成後に事前指定された。治療レジメン推定量は、中間イベントに関わらずすべてのランダム割り付けされた参加者のデータに基づき、主要推定量とされ、有効性推定量は補足とされた。安全性評価項目は、治験薬を少なくとも1回投与されたすべての参加者のデータを使用し、本試験はClinicalTrials.gov（NCT06045221）に登録されており、完了済みである。
abstract_sections.content_en: In this 52-week, randomised, open-label, active-controlled, multicentre, multinational, phase 3 study, we enrolled adults (≥18 years) with type 2 diabetes inadequately controlled with metformin (≥1500 mg per day), glycated haemoglobin (HbA1c) between 7.0% and 10.5% (53–91 mmol/mol), and BMI at least 25 kg/m² from 131 medical research centres and hospitals in Argentina, China, Japan, Mexico, and the USA. Participants were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) to orforglipron (12 mg or 36 mg) or semaglutide (7 mg or 14 mg); all groups had an up to 4-week lead-in period and 52-week treatment period, with the drugs administered orally once per day. The primary objective of the study was to assess non-inferiority of orforglipron 36 mg versus semaglutide 14 mg and orforglipron 12 mg versus semaglutide 7 mg for mean change at week 52 from baseline in HbA1c (with a non-inferiority margin of 0.3%) in the intention-to-treat population. Hierarchical analysis for superiority was prespecified after attainment of non-inferiority. The treatment regimen estimand, based on data from all randomly assigned participants regardless of intercurrent events, was the primary estimand; the efficacy estimand was considered supportive. The safety endpoints used data from all participants who received at least one dose of the study drug. This trial was registered on ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT06045221) and is completed.

abstract_sections.label: 結果
abstract_sections.content: 2023年9月22日から2025年8月22日までに、1698名の参加者が募集され、オルフログリプロン（12 mg群424名、36 mg群423名）またはセマグルチド（7 mg群426名、14 mg群425名）にランダムに割り付けられた。治療レジメン推定量では、ベースラインHbA1c 8.3%からの52週時点での平均変化は、オルフログリプロン12 mg群で-1.71%（SE 0.07）、オルフログリプロン36 mg群で-1.91%（0.08）、セマグルチド7 mg群で-1.23%（0.05）、セマグルチド14 mg群で-1.47%（0.06）であった。推定治療差は、オルフログリプロン12 mg対セマグルチド7 mgで-0.48%（95% CI -0.65～-0.31; p<0.0001）、オルフログリプロン36 mg対セマグルチド14 mgで-0.44%（-0.62～-0.26; p<0.0001）、オルフログリプロン12 mg対セマグルチド14 mgで-0.24%（95% CI -0.41～-0.072; p=0.0050）、オルフログリプロン36 mg対セマグルチド7 mgで-0.68%（-0.85～-0.50; p<0.0001）であった。非劣性という主要目的は達成され、オルフログリプロンの両用量は、オルフログリプロン12 mg対セマグルチド14 mgを含め、両セマグルチド用量に対して優越性を示した。最も頻繁な有害事象は消化管イベントであり（オルフログリプロン12 mg群の424名中249名[59%]、36 mg群の423名中245名[58%]；セマグルチド7 mg群の426名中157名[37%]、14 mg群の425名中193名[45%]）、そのほとんどが軽度から中等度であった。オルフログリプロン群（12 mg群で37名[9%]、36 mg群で41名[10%]）では、セマグルチド群（7 mg群で19名[4%]、14 mg群で21名[5%]）よりも多くの参加者が有害事象のため治験薬を中止し、平均脈拍数増加はオルフログリプロン群（12 mg群3.7拍/分、36 mg群4.7拍/分）の方がセマグルチド群（7 mg群1.0拍/分、14 mg群1.5拍/分）よりも大きかった。研究期間中に4名の死亡が発生した：オルフログリプロン12 mg群で1名、オルフログリプロン36 mg群で1名、セマグルチド7 mg群で2名であった。
abstract_sections.content_en: From Sept 22, 2023, to Aug 22, 2025, 1698 participants were recruited and randomly assigned to orforglipron (n=424 on 12 mg and n=423 on 36 mg) or semaglutide (n=426 on 7 mg and n=425 on 14 mg). For the treatment regimen estimand, mean changes at week 52 from a baseline HbA1c of 8.3% were –1.71% (SE 0.07) with orforglipron 12 mg, –1.91% (0.08) with orforglipron 36 mg, –1.23% (0.05) with semaglutide 7 mg, and –1.47% (0.06) with semaglutide 14 mg. Estimated treatment differences were –0.48% (95% CI –0.65 to –0.31; p<0.0001) for orforglipron 12 mg versus semaglutide 7 mg; –0.44% (–0.62 to –0·26; p<0.0001) for orforglipron 36 mg versus semaglutide 14 mg; –0.24% (95% CI –0.41 to –0.072; p=0.0050) for orforglipron 12 mg versus semaglutide 14 mg; and –0.68% (–0.85 to –0.50; p<0.0001) for orforglipron 36 mg versus semaglutide 7 mg. The primary objective of non-inferiority was met and both orforglipron doses showed superiority to both semaglutide doses, including orforglipron 12 mg versus semaglutide 14 mg. The most frequent adverse events were gastrointestinal events (orforglipron: 249 [59%] of 424 on 12 mg and 245 [58%] of 423 on 36 mg; semaglutide: 157 [37%] of 426 on 7 mg and 193 [45%] of 425 on 14 mg), most of which were mild to moderate in severity. More participants in the orforglipron groups (37 [9%] on 12 mg and 41 [10%] on 36 mg) discontinued study treatment due to adverse events than in the semaglutide groups (19 (4%) on 7 mg and 21 (5%) on 14 mg), and mean increase in pulse rate was greater in the orforglipron groups (12 mg 3.7 bpm; 36 mg 4.7 bpm) than in the semaglutide groups (7 mg 1.0 bpm; 14 mg 1.5 bpm). Four deaths occurred during the study: one in the orforglipron 12 mg group, one in the orforglipron 36 mg group, and two in the semaglutide 7 mg group.

abstract_sections.label: 結論
abstract_sections.content: メトホルミンで不十分にコントロールされている2型糖尿病患者において、オルフログリプロン12 mgと36 mgは、ベースラインからのHbA1cの52週時点での平均変化に関して、セマグルチド7 mgおよび14 mgに対して非劣性であり、優越性を示した。両オルフログリプロンおよびセマグルチドの安全性プロファイルはGLP-1受容体作動薬クラスと概ね一致していたが、消化管イベントの発生率、有害事象による中止率、および平均脈拍数増加は、経口セマグルチドと比較してオルフログリプロンで高かった。
abstract_sections.content_en: In individuals with type 2 diabetes inadequately controlled with metformin, orforglipron 12 mg and 36 mg was non-inferior and superior to semaglutide 7 mg and 14 mg with respect to the mean change in HbA1c from baseline to 52 weeks. Although the safety profiles of both orforglipron and semaglutide were generally consistent with the GLP-1 receptor agonist class, the incidence of gastrointestinal events, discontinuations due to adverse events, and mean increase in pulse rate were higher with orforglipron than oral semaglutide.

pico.P: メトホルミン（1日1500mg以上）で不十分にコントロールされた2型糖尿病の成人患者（18歳以上、HbA1c 7.0%～10.5% [53～91 mmol/mol]、BMI 25 kg/m²以上）。
pico.I: オルフログリプロン（12 mgまたは36 mg）を1日1回経口投与。
pico.C: 経口セマグルチド（7 mgまたは14 mg）を1日1回経口投与。
pico.O: 主要評価項目は、ベースラインからの52週時点でのHbA1cの平均変化。オルフログリプロンは非劣性を達成し、両用量ともにセマグルチドの両用量に対して優越性を示した。オルフログリプロン12 mg vs セマグルチド7 mgで-0.48% (95% CI -0.65～-0.31; p<0.0001)、オルフログリプロン36 mg vs セマグルチド14 mgで-0.44% (-0.62～-0.26; p<0.0001)、オルフログリプロン12 mg vs セマグルチド14 mgで-0.24% (95% CI -0.41～-0.072; p=0.0050)、オルフログリプロン36 mg vs セマグルチド7 mgで-0.68% (-0.85～-0.50; p<0.0001)の治療差が推定された。消化管イベントの発生率はオルフログリプロン群（58-59%）でセマグルチド群（37-45%）より高く、有害事象による治験中止率（オルフログリプロン群9-10% vs セマグルチド群4-5%）も高かった。平均脈拍数増加はオルフログリプロン群（12 mgで3.7拍/分、36 mgで4.7拍/分）の方がセマグルチド群（7 mgで1.0拍/分、14 mgで1.5拍/分）よりも大きかった。

papers.title: Three Low-Dose Antihypertensive Agents in a Single Pill after Intracerebral Hemorrhage
papers.journal: The NEW ENGLAND JOURNAL of MEDICINE
papers.authors: Anderson CS, Song L, et al.
papers.abstract_en:
BACKGROUND
Blood-pressure reduction is the only proven treatment to prevent stroke due to intracerebral hemorrhage. Whether a single pill that combines three antihypertensive drugs at low doses, in addition to standard antihypertensive treatment, can lower blood pressure more than standard care alone and reduce the risk of recurrent stroke after intracerebral hemorrhage is uncertain.
METHODS
We conducted a multinational, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial involving patients with a history of intracerebral hemorrhage. Patients were eligible for the trial if they had a systolic blood pressure of 130 to 160 mm Hg at baseline and were in clinically stable condition. After a 2-week active run-in phase during which all the patients received a once-daily pill containing three antihypertensive agents at low doses (telmisartan at 20 mg, amlodipine at 2.5 mg, and indapamide at 1.25 mg; the triple pill), the patients were randomly assigned to continue receiving the triple pill or to receive matching placebo. The primary outcome was the first recurrent stroke. Secondary outcomes included blood-pressure control, major cardiovascular events, death from cardiovascular causes, and safety.
RESULTS
Of 1670 patients who underwent randomization, 833 were assigned to receive the triple pill and 837 to receive placebo. The mean age of the patients was 58 years. At a median follow-up of 2.5 years, recurrent stroke had occurred in 38 patients (4.6%) in the triple-pill group and 62 (7.4%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.61; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.41 to 0.92; P=0.02). The mean systolic blood pressure during follow-up was 127 mm Hg and 138 mm Hg, respectively. The incidence of major cardiovascular events was lower with the triple pill than with placebo (6.6% vs. 9.8%; P=0.04). Serious adverse events occurred in 23.2% of the patients in the triple-pill group and 26.0% of those in the placebo group. Early discontinuation of the trial regimen due to an adverse event occurred in 13.6% and 6.0%, respectively. The most common adverse event leading to discontinuation was an increase of 20% or more in the serum creatinine level.
CONCLUSIONS
Among patients with intracerebral hemorrhage, treatment with a combination of three low-dose antihypertensive agents in a single pill, in addition to standard care, was associated with a lower incidence of recurrent stroke and major cardiovascular events than placebo. (Funded by the National Health and Medical Research Council of Australia and the Brazilian Ministry of Health; TRIDENT ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT02699645; Australian New Zealand Clinical Trials Registry number, ACTRN12616000327482.)

abstract_sections.label: 背景
abstract_sections.content: 脳内出血による脳卒中を予防するための唯一の確立された治療法は、血圧低下である。3種類の降圧薬を低用量で組み合わせた単一ピルが、標準的な降圧治療に加えて、標準治療単独よりも血圧をさらに低下させ、脳内出血後の再発性脳卒中のリスクを減少させられるかは不明である。
abstract_sections.content_en: Blood-pressure reduction is the only proven treatment to prevent stroke due to intracerebral hemorrhage. Whether a single pill that combines three antihypertensive drugs at low doses, in addition to standard antihypertensive treatment, can lower blood pressure more than standard care alone and reduce the risk of recurrent stroke after intracerebral hemorrhage is uncertain.

abstract_sections.label: 方法
abstract_sections.content: 脳内出血の既往がある患者を対象に、多国籍、二重盲検、無作為化、プラセボ対照試験を実施した。患者は、ベースライン時の収縮期血圧が130～160 mmHgで臨床的に安定している場合に試験の対象とされた。2週間のアクティブ・ランイン期間中、全患者が低用量の3種類の降圧薬（テルミサルタン20 mg、アムロジピン2.5 mg、インダパミド1.25 mg；トリプルピル）を含む1日1回服用ピルを服用した後、患者はトリプルピルの継続投与群または一致するプラセボ投与群に無作為に割り付けられた。主要評価項目は初回再発性脳卒中とした。副次評価項目には、血圧コントロール、主要心血管イベント、心血管原因による死亡、安全性を含めた。
abstract_sections.content_en: We conducted a multinational, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial involving patients with a history of intracerebral hemorrhage. Patients were eligible for the trial if they had a systolic blood pressure of 130 to 160 mm Hg at baseline and were in clinically stable condition. After a 2-week active run-in phase during which all the patients received a once-daily pill containing three antihypertensive agents at low doses (telmisartan at 20 mg, amlodipine at 2.5 mg, and indapamide at 1.25 mg; the triple pill), the patients were randomly assigned to continue receiving the triple pill or to receive matching placebo. The primary outcome was the first recurrent stroke. Secondary outcomes included blood-pressure control, major cardiovascular events, death from cardiovascular causes, and safety.

abstract_sections.label: 結果
abstract_sections.content: 無作為化された1670人の患者のうち、833人がトリプルピル群に、837人がプラセボ群に割り付けられた。患者の平均年齢は58歳であった。追跡期間中央値2.5年で、再発性脳卒中はトリプルピル群で38人（4.6%）、プラセボ群で62人（7.4%）に発生した（ハザード比0.61；95%信頼区間0.41～0.92；P=0.02）。追跡期間中の平均収縮期血圧は、それぞれ127 mmHgと138 mmHgであった。主要心血管イベントの発生率は、トリプルピル群がプラセボ群よりも低かった（6.6%対9.8%；P=0.04）。重篤な有害事象は、トリプルピル群の患者の23.2%とプラセボ群の患者の26.0%に発生した。有害事象による治験レジメンの早期中止は、それぞれ13.6%と6.0%に発生した。中止に至った最も一般的な有害事象は、血清クレアチニンレベルが20%以上増加したことであった。
abstract_sections.content_en: Of 1670 patients who underwent randomization, 833 were assigned to receive the triple pill and 837 to receive placebo. The mean age of the patients was 58 years. At a median follow-up of 2.5 years, recurrent stroke had occurred in 38 patients (4.6%) in the triple-pill group and 62 (7.4%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.61; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.41 to 0.92; P=0.02). The mean systolic blood pressure during follow-up was 127 mm Hg and 138 mm Hg, respectively. The incidence of major cardiovascular events was lower with the triple pill than with placebo (6.6% vs. 9.8%; P=0.04). Serious adverse events occurred in 23.2% of the patients in the triple-pill group and 26.0% of those in the placebo group. Early discontinuation of the trial regimen due to an adverse event occurred in 13.6% and 6.0%, respectively. The most common adverse event leading to discontinuation was an increase of 20% or more in the serum creatinine level.

abstract_sections.label: 結論
abstract_sections.content: 脳内出血患者において、3種類の低用量降圧薬を単一ピルとして標準治療に追加することは、プラセボと比較して、再発性脳卒中および主要心血管イベントの発生率の低下と関連していた。（オーストラリア国立保健医療研究評議会およびブラジル保健省の資金提供による；TRIDENT ClinicalTrials.gov番号、NCT02699645；Australian New Zealand Clinical Trials Registry番号、ACTRN12616000327482。）
abstract_sections.content_en: Among patients with intracerebral hemorrhage, treatment with a combination of three low-dose antihypertensive agents in a single pill, in addition to standard care, was associated with a lower incidence of recurrent stroke and major cardiovascular events than placebo. (Funded by the National Health and Medical Research Council of Australia and the Brazilian Ministry of Health; TRIDENT ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT02699645; Australian New Zealand Clinical Trials Registry number, ACTRN12616000327482.)

pico.P: 脳内出血の既往があり、ベースライン時の収縮期血圧が130～160 mmHgで臨床的に安定している患者 (Patients with a history of intracerebral hemorrhage, with a systolic blood pressure of 130 to 160 mm Hg at baseline and in clinically stable condition)
pico.I: 標準治療に加え、低用量の3種類の降圧薬（テルミサルタン20 mg、アムロジピン2.5 mg、インダパミド1.25 mg；トリプルピル）を含む1日1回服用ピル (A once-daily pill containing three low-dose antihypertensive agents (telmisartan 20 mg, amlodipine 2.5 mg, and indapamide 1.25 mg; the triple pill), in addition to standard care)
pico.C: 一致するプラセボ (Matching placebo)
pico.O: 初回再発性脳卒中：トリプルピル群で38人（4.6%）、プラセボ群で62人（7.4%）に発生した（ハザード比0.61；95%信頼区間0.41～0.92；P=0.02）。(First recurrent stroke: occurred in 38 patients (4.6%) in the triple-pill group and 62 (7.4%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.41 to 0.92; P=0.02).)
